description: Data dictionary for a GitHub-ready e-commerce analytics project covering MySQL import, SQL cleaning, KPI analysis, RFM segmentation, fulfillment analysis, and review analysis.

grain_definition.orders: One row represents one unique order_id.
grain_definition.order_items: One row represents one product line item within one order_id, identified by order_id + order_item_id.
grain_definition.customers: One row represents one customer record identified by customer_id. A customer_unique_id can map to multiple customer_id records across orders.
grain_definition.products: One row represents one unique product_id.
grain_definition.reviews: One row represents one review record identified by review_id, linked to an order_id. In practice, review_id may repeat across orders in some source versions, so order-level joins should be validated carefully.
grain_definition.payments: One row represents one payment installment or payment method record for one order_id. One order can have multiple payment rows.

fields.description: Unique identifier of the order.
fields.business_role: Primary key; core join key for order-level analysis.

fields.description: Identifier of the customer record associated with the order.
fields.business_role: Foreign key to customers; used for buyer and repeat purchase analysis.

fields.description: Current status of the order, such as delivered, shipped, canceled, or unavailable.
fields.business_role: Business filter field; defines valid completed orders for KPI analysis.

fields.description: Timestamp when the order was placed by the customer.
fields.business_role: Primary time field for GMV, Orders, Buyers, and trend analysis.

fields.description: Timestamp when the order payment was approved.
fields.business_role: Operational milestone; used to analyze approval lag and funnel progression.

fields.description: Timestamp when the order was handed over to the logistics carrier.
fields.business_role: Fulfillment milestone; used in shipping lead time analysis.

fields.description: Timestamp when the order was delivered to the customer.
fields.business_role: Delivery completion field; used for fulfillment and late delivery analysis.

fields.description: Estimated delivery date promised to the customer.
fields.business_role: Benchmark field for late delivery and customer experience analysis.

tables.grain_definition: One row per order item line, uniquely identified by order_id + order_item_id.

fields.description: Identifier of the order containing the item.
fields.business_role: Foreign key to orders; used to aggregate item-level revenue to order-level metrics.

fields.description: Sequential item number within an order.
fields.business_role: Line-item grain field; supports item count and duplicate line validation.

fields.description: Identifier of the purchased product.
fields.business_role: Foreign key to products; supports product and category analysis.

fields.description: Identifier of the seller fulfilling the item.
fields.business_role: Operational dimension; can support seller-level analysis if extended later.

fields.description: Deadline for the seller to ship the item.
fields.business_role: Operational SLA field; useful for fulfillment timeliness checks.

fields.description: Item selling price excluding freight.
fields.business_role: Core revenue component; primary basis for GMV in this project.

fields.description: Freight amount charged for the item.
fields.business_role: Logistics cost / customer charge field; may be included separately in extended revenue analysis.

tables.grain_definition: One row per customer_id. customer_unique_id represents the real-world customer entity across multiple orders.

fields.description: Order-level customer record identifier.
fields.business_role: Primary join key to orders.

fields.description: Stable unique identifier for the actual customer across multiple customer_id records.
fields.business_role: Canonical customer key; used for Buyers, Repeat Rate, and RFM analysis.

fields.description: Prefix of the customer's ZIP code.
fields.business_role: Geographic dimension; used for regional segmentation if needed.

fields.description: City of the customer.
fields.business_role: Location dimension; used for city-level distribution analysis.

fields.description: State of the customer.
fields.business_role: Core geographic dimension for buyer distribution and market coverage analysis.

fields.description: Unique identifier of the product.
fields.business_role: Primary join key from order_items.

fields.description: Original product category name.
fields.business_role: Product dimension; used for category-level performance analysis.

fields.description: Length of the product name text in characters.
fields.business_role: Data quality / catalog attribute; low direct business priority.

fields.description: Length of the product description text in characters.
fields.business_role: Catalog richness attribute; useful for extended content-quality analysis.

fields.description: Number of product photos available.
fields.business_role: Catalog presentation attribute; can support conversion-quality exploration.

fields.description: Product weight in grams.
fields.business_role: Logistics attribute; may affect freight and delivery experience.

fields.description: Product length in centimeters.
fields.business_role: Physical attribute; useful for logistics and packaging analysis.

fields.description: Product height in centimeters.
fields.business_role: Physical attribute; useful for logistics and packaging analysis.

fields.description: Product width in centimeters.
fields.business_role: Physical attribute; useful for logistics and packaging analysis.

tables.grain_definition: One row per review event linked to an order_id; validate duplicates before aggregation.

fields.description: Identifier of the review record.
fields.business_role: Review event identifier; used for deduplication checks.

fields.description: Order identifier linked to the review.
fields.business_role: Foreign key to orders; supports order experience analysis.

fields.description: Customer rating score, typically from 1 to 5.
fields.business_role: Primary customer satisfaction metric.

fields.description: Short title entered by the reviewer.
fields.business_role: Qualitative feedback attribute; optional for text analysis.

fields.description: Detailed comment entered by the reviewer.
fields.business_role: Qualitative feedback attribute; optional for NLP or sentiment extensions.

fields.description: Date when the review was created.
fields.business_role: Time field for review trend analysis.

fields.description: Timestamp when the review was answered or recorded.
fields.business_role: Operational response timing field.

tables.grain_definition: One row per order payment record; one order may have multiple installments or payment method records.

fields.description: Identifier of the order associated with the payment.
fields.business_role: Foreign key to orders; used to reconcile payment values at order level.

fields.description: Sequence number of the payment record within the order.
fields.business_role: Line sequencing field; supports multi-payment validation.

fields.description: Method used for payment, such as credit_card, voucher, or boleto.
fields.business_role: Payment channel dimension; useful for payment mix analysis.

fields.description: Number of installments used in the payment.
fields.business_role: Payment behavior field; useful for customer payment preference analysis.

fields.description: Payment amount recorded for the payment entry.
fields.business_role: Order settlement amount; used for payment reconciliation and validation against item totals.

core_metrics.grain_definition: Usually aggregated by day, month, customer segment, or overall business period based on delivered orders.
core_metrics.definition: Gross Merchandise Value calculated as the sum of item selling price from valid completed orders.
core_metrics.business_rule: Recommended to include only delivered orders and exclude canceled or unavailable orders. Freight is excluded from the default GMV definition in this project.

core_metrics.grain_definition: Counted at order_id grain and then aggregated by time or segment.
core_metrics.definition: Number of unique valid orders in the selected analysis scope.
core_metrics.business_rule: Recommended to count only delivered orders for comparable KPI tracking unless a broader funnel analysis is explicitly needed.

core_metrics.grain_definition: Counted at customer_unique_id grain and then aggregated by time or segment.
core_metrics.definition: Number of unique purchasing customers in the selected analysis scope.
core_metrics.business_rule: Use customer_unique_id instead of customer_id to avoid overcounting the same real-world customer across multiple order records.

core_metrics.grain_definition: Calculated over an order-level scope for a selected time window or segment.
core_metrics.definition: Average Order Value, representing average GMV per valid order.
core_metrics.business_rule: Keep the numerator and denominator under the same order-status filter. Default project logic uses delivered orders only.

core_metrics.grain_definition: Calculated at customer_unique_id grain within a selected analysis period.
core_metrics.definition: Share of buyers who placed at least two valid orders.
core_metrics.formula: COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN customer_order_cnt >= 2 THEN customer_unique_id END) / COUNT(DISTINCT customer_unique_id)
core_metrics.business_rule: Customer order count should be built using customer_unique_id and valid completed orders only.

core_metrics.grain_definition: Calculated at order_id grain and then aggregated by time, region, or review segment.
core_metrics.definition: Proportion of delivered orders where actual delivery date is later than estimated delivery date.
core_metrics.formula: COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN orders.order_delivered_customer_date > orders.order_estimated_delivery_date THEN orders.order_id END) / COUNT(DISTINCT orders.order_id)
core_metrics.business_rule: Only evaluate delivered orders with non-null actual and estimated delivery timestamps.

analysis_notes: Grain consistency is critical. Do not aggregate GMV or Orders directly from joined tables without checking for one-to-many duplication.
Use orders as the primary fact grain for order-level KPIs and join order_items only when revenue components are needed.
Use customer_unique_id, not customer_id, for customer lifecycle, repeat purchase, and RFM analysis.
Validate review and payment table duplication before combining them with item-level data.
All KPI definitions should explicitly state included order statuses to keep SQL, notebooks, and README conclusions aligned.
